title: Treasury Executive Office for Asset Forfeiture (TEOAF)
summary: The Treasury Executive Office for Asset Forfeiture (TEOAF) is a Treasury administrative office established in 1992 that manages the Treasury Forfeiture Fund (TFF), a special federal fund for deposit of non-tax forfeitures from Treasury and Department of Homeland Security law enforcement agencies. The TFF operates pursuant to Title 31 U.S.C. § 9705 and does not require annual congressional appropriation to allocate funds.
key_points: TEOAF was established in 1992 to influence consistent and strategic use of asset forfeiture by law enforcement
The TFF is a special fund (earmarked by law for specific purposes) governed by Title 31 U.S.C. § 9705
Participating agencies include IRS-CI, CBP, HSI, USSS, USCG, FLETC, FinCEN, and TTB
Asset forfeiture aims to deprive criminals of proceeds, disrupt criminal enterprises, and compensate victims
TFF funds can be allocated without annual congressional appropriation
TEOAF priorities include fiscal responsibility, due process protection, targeting high-profile criminals, and fostering federal-state partnerships
